reviews.review_description:
If you liked F-ZERO, back in the day, on the SNES, or WIPEOUT on the PS1, this is going to be your favorite VR title for a while. It's that kind of mechanic & aesthetic, realized pretty perfectly in VR. Graphics are smooth & slick, the sense of motion is fast and exhilarating, and the interface, reward system, & build trees are well designed & thoughtful.  

Getting YOUR specific set up dialed in, figuring out where you like the bike to sit (it's helpfully adjustable by grabbing and placing before each race), and experimenting with the different throttle & steering choices will take a little bit of time-- and until you do, by trial and error, you'll feel like you can't control anything. But once you click with it, or once it clicks, the reward of control and power is pretty greaf. Took me about 25 minutes before my "aha!"  monent came. But that first race where I "got" the controls and finished in 1st place was amazing. And addictive. 

If you're looking to add a racing game to your VR collection, this is the one.
this one's been a lot of fun so far. get the quest games optimizer app and you can run this at 150% resolution and 120hz. this makes for butter smooth game play and sharp clarity.

reviews.review_title: Best Racing Game on the Quest.
reviews.review_description: It's amazing in every way. Absolutely beautiful levels. Tons of options for bike handling and customizations. A large selection of tracks. I remember playing Excitebike as a kid in the 80s. This is light years ahead of that. I highly recommend this game.

developer_response.body: Hey punkemoska, thanks for taking the time to write such a glowing review for us!

reviews.review_description: Fast, responsive, fun.

reviews.review_title: VR Game of the Year. No question.
reviews.review_description:
Before I get into why exactly this is my VR GOTY pick, I’ll make it simple: 
 
If you’re looking for a Quest racing game, this is the one to get. Period. Trust me. It’s head and shoulders above everything else, and it’s the best VR game I’ve played in 2025. Get it. 
 
***********
 
Now, for the longer review: 
 
In terms of comfort, physics, replayability, and overall fun, the difference between this game and all other (non-PCVR) Quest racing games is exponential. Why? Because they knew exactly what they were going for, and got all of it exactly right. I mean, these devs NAILED IT. This is a perfect mixture of simulation physics and arcade gameplay, with incredible bike handling, fun race modes, and zero fat. 
 
It never tries to overstep its boundaries or get cute, instead focusing on the bike’s ridiculously addictive and accurate handling and the type of “career arcade” experience that I’ve sorely missed in modern, bloated racing games. In a racing game, I don’t need or want a bunch of incremental RPG-style grinding tuneups, or some stupid half-baked “world” where I have to walk around and talk to NPCs. 
 
All I want in a racing game is to put the headset on and race, while feeling exactly like I’m actually on a motorcycle. (Or, in this case, a hoverbike). And let me tell you, this game delivers. What VRacer Hoverbike reminds me the most of is Need For Speed Hot Pursuit (which is high praise, given that the remastered version is my favorite racing game of all time). I’ve missed games like that. 
 
But really, it’s all about the bike physics, which are spectacular. As someone who used to ride a motorcycle, this is the first VR game where I’ve actually felt like it’s an actual bike, and my real-world skills carry over. Is it a perfect motorcycle training sim? No, because that’s not the point — it’s a hoverbike! But dear Lord, what a hoverbike. It is SO MUCH FUN TO RIDE. 
 
And while I was initially confused by the chest lean + countersteering controls (devs, I’m curious: in chest lean+horizontal steering bar mode, when you “turn the handlebars,” why does the hoverbike turn the opposite direction an actual motorcycle would lol?), I eventually settled on the vertical steering bar + chest lean at a 70/30 ratio in favor of the handlebars. But within 20 minutes, it all felt natural. 
 
I really only have one more note for the devs, this game is near perfect…: 
 
Devs, could you please add a “Training / Free Track Mode,” where we can mess around with the calibration, steering mode ratios, and seating, both while staying still and also free-driving around an empty track? It’s tough to tell which setup is actually the best if I’m not able to comparison test them in real time. That would be a huge help. 
 
In conclusion: this is by far the best VR game I’ve played all year in any genre. It is the best racing game I’ve ever played without a racing wheel setup, and is an absolute must-buy for all Quest owners. 
 
9.7/10
Great game! makes you feel like you are really flying down the track
Feels abandoned. Absolutely nobody online for multiplayer.  Game is actually well made..

This game is different from… but really familiar to… one of my most loved games. I was really disappointed to find out that PS was NOT upgrading one of the best VR games in their catalog to the new PSVR2. 

If you’re jonesing for that adrenaline rush of high speed hover racing… this is it!

This is by far the best motorbike experience on quest 3 standalone,  yes technically is a hooverbike (flying close to the ground ) but feel exactly like a moto gp bikes ! 
Now let me explain you why this game is so good because nobody explain this and it is very important to know 

The main reason is the way you control the bikes , the leaning controlls is one of the most ingenious and innovative control system of the bikes ever made because add a tone of realism weight and inerzia to the bikes ! You really feel on a real bike when you lean into the turns , you have to be also very precise because you cannot lean left to right in milliseconds,  takes a little time , so this feature make the game feels very simulative 

Anyway the controlls are fully customizable,  you can also turn in the classic way or even a mix of leaning and classic mode 

Great tracks , great customization , great graphics,  carrier , sound , frame rate , everything works great,  the textures of the environments are a little soft , but doesn't really matter because you are focused on the tracks at 250 miles /h 

You have the option also to change the class of the bikes like 250 .450 cc  or more 

If you love motorbikes this ia the game , the best of them all and I own more than 150 games
Awesome game, Wipeout Vibes but on a bike with immersive leaning control, what I’ve needed on the the Quest and in life 😃😂👍

developer_response.body: Hey StewPendus13, good to know that VRacer Hoverbike is hitting the right spot for you! Have fun and see you on the tracks 🏍️

reviews.review_description:
I’m really enjoying playing this! 
I’ve completed every race, gold medal in all.
There’s plenty to do, it’s no small little game.
I’ve just started getting into the online side of it.
The weekly online challenges and the leaderboard are great too, I highly reccomend getting this! 
Back in the day, this is what I wished wipeout was.

TO THE DEVS,
If there was anything I would ask for it would be a way to toggle on/off which songs come on, out of what is in the playlist.
And to be able to choose which song comes on at the start of a race in the options.
For me this would just make everything perfect.
Perhaps a few more drum and bass style faster BPM tunes too, it just makes the racing feel faster.

*The music is great, and the racing defo feels fast!

Good fun, and they have figured out comfortable bike controls! This was my biggest question previous to making the purchase, and they nailed it. They have given us four different options to steer: head tilt, two variations using your controllers like handlebars, and finally, your thumbsticks. If you liked Wipeout, you will like this.

This is one of the best games on Quest. The mechanics are buttery smooth and satisfying, the progression is the right balance of fun and engaging while not feeling overwhelming and the graphics and overall art direction are amazing. It all comes together for a solid AAA experience I’d personally recommend to anyone with even a passing interest. I don’t think you’ll be disappointed.

My only complaint is I can’t figure out how to steer with the controllers, they seem to be inverted.

But I’m able to use the chest steer just fine.
All in all, great game no other complaints.

There are many times when in gameplay where there are ghost inputs, such as being off the throttle and the vehicle keeps accelerating, or the inputs act as through they are stuck in a specific direction; vehicle is turning to the left, lean right and the vehicle continues going left. There is a plethora of control options, headset lean w/ thresholds, countersteer with controllers in the y/z axis w/ sensitivity, throttle with triggers or z-twist, and muxing between the two. Constantly adjusting the settings to try and get a functional setup for inputs while in gameplay and the inputs only seem to work 80% of the time, other times it's as if the input system takes a nap. This is with a brand new headset, fresh hand controller batteries, lights on or off in multiples rooms of the house.  Dropping down to two stars - the game is playable but the aforementioned issues make playing the game very frustrating as it's near impossible to succeed.

developer_response.body: Please come to our discord so we can figure this out. We have had a couple of players who reported similar problems appearing out of nowhere after playing the game without any problem for months (it turned out it had to do with an OS update issue that has since been fixed)

reviews.review_description: Just stsrted playing, having a ball so far. Outstanding immersive experiece. Highly recommend

Fast, fun, hard, satisfying to learn, with a nice sensation of speed. Play and sleep and play again and you will see progress. 

I am about an hour into the single player campaign and it is super fun. I will play this like crazy to learn all the tracks before I try online though 😆. Can't wait till I am good enough!!

It needs head inertia physics, though, especially for fast transitions like jumps and loops, to take the pukey feeling away.

I rarely keep games but this is too good.

By far, the best teaching game so far for quest.  The visuals are great,  there isn't too much grinding to progress, but you do have to earn it, it's pitched perfectly.  I just need more practice to improve lap times and I'll be ready for MP racing without looking like a noob.  Can't wait to see more stuff to come with this game.  It's deffo a keeper!
This is a childhood dream coming true. As a wipeout fusion fan on playstation 2 back in the days, this is exactly what I  was looking for. Thanks devs!

developer_response.body: You must have guessed we are WipeOut fans too ;-)

reviews.review_description: pretty good so far. A bit more sensitive than a real bike but great fun

This game has the best set of controls I’ve ever seen in a racing game. There is so much off line content to keep any racing fan happy, and there is more to come. There are 3 different movement settings to choose from. I recommend trying them all to which one suits your play style. It has different classes of cc to choose from each one going up makes the bikes faster. I does take a bit of practice. I do recommend this game to any one who likes bike,car, or sim racing games. Also if you want there is a great multiplayer game here as well.

My favorite combat racing game to date. Every race is absolute chaos. The graphics are really nice and the game runs smoothly, not that you have a lot of time to notice. Great game to just drop in for a multiplayer race on a whim and every race is total chaotic fun.

Such a fun thrill ride of a game paired with depth and nuances that will keep you busy trying to improve. Great range of difficulty from accessible earlier tracks to crazy hard technical tracks. Something for everyone! I can’t get enough, highly recommended this feels like the next big thing for vr.

I took advantage of the sale to buy this yesterday after it being on my wishlist for a while, and I am well happy that I did.. The graphics are superb, the speed is awesome too.. You can control your hoverbike in a number of ways (3) I like to lean into the turn and also use the bars, the controls seem very intuitive and I was flying round the tracks after a few attempts. I definitely would recommend this game to anyone who loves a racing title and has maybe played Wipeout 👌

I was trying to figure out how to praise this game as it truly is incredible, but then I saw moose moose' review & thought that person nailed it.. Anyway, it's a great game, guys!! (You should get Star Wars license & do the pod racer stuff..!)

I bought this game exclusively for multiplayer races, I've been playing Wipeout since 1997 and recently Wipeout PS VR. I regretted paying for this game that doesn't have any players in it. I only beat some once in the summer. Too bad. I don't know and I don't think it's the fault of the game's programmers, maybe people don't play competitive futuristic speed games. If the programmers know what day people enter, the information would be welcome. And depending on whether I see that games are being made and the game has novice drivers and not players, the programmers themselves, I will change my review.

developer_response.body:
Hey, if you are into multiplayer, come to our discord. https://discord.gg/4xtbRUzC
Multiplayer is mostly active in the evening and people ping each other on discord to hop in. The game community is growing with the recent release on PSVR2. Help us pushing it so it's easier for you to find people to play with!

reviews.review_description: Thanks a lot for that game, it is smooth and the head and lean movement to turn is super addictive and precise. On top of a good solo experience, the multiplayer is working perfectly with fast loading and proper options to create private rooms. I created a public room to see how is the mood and only chill and calm people joined us, we had good fun immediately and it is rare in vr, since so much years there is not enough cool multiplayer games since echo vr. (of course walkabout is good, eleven table is good but vracer is super welcome !)

The diversity of ways to ride the bike is awesome. They all have their own charm depending on where you are playing. Thumbs up to the développers. It plays like a glove. Speed sensation is great. I just need a fan on my face synchronised to the speed. Some tracks could be a little better though.

I'm dropping 2 stars because even though the game is very enjoyable, the graphics are a huge let down for quest 3/3s users. These honestly look like quest 1 graphics to me and it really ruins my immersion. Maybe it was deliberate to keep frames high and as stable as possible since it's a high speed racing game? Whatever the reason, the graphics are such an eye sore that I can't enjoy it the way I really want to
An absolute blast to play.  Love that they have haptic support.  Very glad I bought on impulse.  Well worth the price.

THIS GAME ROCKS!! totally mind shocked it's been a long time since I had a wow moment in a vr game but this definitely gave me one then two then three!!!  I've only ever used standalone vr just to let ya know!! I hope this game gets all the love it deserves for the love careb and attention that has obviously been blessed upon this game and the little touches that just chefs kiss it on top! Also I did not receive any motion sickness which I can be prone too lol can't play star wars galaxies edge for god's sakes it's so random!! Jokes aside DEVS  you definitely earned the praise for this Betty from me I hope this is just the beginning of this games journey!!! 😁😁😁😁 The save glitch has been fixed now!! At least give the trial ago as I know most will definitely keep this one!!😁😁 And please leave a review so that others can see this game is awesome so it gets a place on the storefront get some of that shovelware off!! 😂🤣🤣 Also don't forget to join the vertexbreakers discord channel to find all the players can't wait to see you in there!!! 😁 so that people know most players start playing between 7pm and 8pm uk time plus its now crossplay come help grow the community😀😀
Awesome game! The controls are done well and the graphics are very good.
Been addicted to this game!
Great sense of speed, nice visuals & plenty of control options.

Love this game! Great speed and loads of customization. One player mode deep and engaging. Different driving modes make your racing preferences personal and immersive. Not the greatest graphics, but the immersion and playability will keep you coming back for more!👏🏻👍🏻💯

Lately, I have been really disheartened by the current state of VR.  I will keep this short with this: buy this game.  Congratulations to everyone who worked on this.  Slam-freaking-dunk.  Thank you very, very, very much.  -Kenneth

i have tried all control types and none feel good. Cant even complete the fourth race in carreer because of the controls. Have finished fzero gx and whipe out but cant get anywhere here. No idea what i am doing wrong but i cant like the game like this. Everyone else seems to love it so that a good thing i gues.

developer_response.body: Feel free to join our discord if you want help. Most players like the controls so maybe there is something going wrong for some reason. https://discord.gg/ywbFgnUu

reviews.review_description: Wipe out nostalgy

reviews.review_title: Best handling in any VR game.
reviews.review_description: This game is a must play for any racing fan, and even if your not the biggest racing fan I'd say give it a shot. It's pretty awesome fun, with great graphics and a great community. Lobbies usually have players unlike other VR online games, and even if it's quiet the single player and weekly challenge will keep you occupied.

So far, I'm really digging this game...there's three ways you can control your bike ...I've only used the leaning option...absolutely love that method.

The tracks I've played so far are a lot of fun.

Really glad I pulled the trigger on this one.
so much fun best vr race game
Though not much of a racing game fan, this one got me hooked. Would love to see even more weapons and bike designs in future updates
Didn't know if i would like this Game, but turns out its great. Really like everything about it. Hard to stop playing once i get started. Worth getting i think.
Excellent graphics, great sense of speed, lots of tracks and environments, plenty of race modes, very good bHaptics support. Just a fun game overall.
This game is fantastic for all ages,. so much fun with a great online community. Get it you won't regret it
I'm reminded of the first time I sat on a Sega Hang-On full-size bike, aged 6 or 7. I left that experience much the same as this, with a smile from ear to ear.

reviews.review_title: I've got them both on meta and Steam.
reviews.review_description: god fun with friends or even yourself.  my title already explained how I enjoy it.

I remember playing this on the Ogo and it was one of my favourite games. I spent hours just racing around the tracks just because it felt incredible.

I’m so glad to see how far the game has come. It looks and feels amazing and the additional features are the cherry on top. 

This is to the Quest what wipeout was to the Playstation and I hope the Devs get their flowers and lots of money for their efforts. 

Thanks

Internet IS required. 
 Unless you want to start with the training level every time you play.
 Had promise. Im returning it.

developer_response.body: Internet is only required the first time you start the game. After that you can play the game offline with no problem. You just won’t get online leaderboards and multiplayer but all the other modes of the game are playable offline

reviews.review_title: Best racing game on Quest so far
reviews.review_description:
VRacer is a mix of wipeout and mario kart in VR. You control your bike with leaning and controller gestures and it works really well. VRacer has alot of different modes like career, combat, time trial, multiplayer and more. You can raise your level, earn money and buy new bikes and cosmetics. The multiplayer is excellent too, its basically a serious mario kart on bikes with power ups (when you choose combat) and its alot of fun. 
For me its one of the best racing games for vr and when you have the slightest interest in racing games, you should absolutely buy it.

developer_response.body: "Mario Kart on bikes". We are honored :-)

reviews.review_description: Nothing more to add, get it!
Fast, addictive and highly polished game.

Excellent game, nice graphics nice music, and an absolutely fantastic sense of speed. Like others have pointed out, the controls are very intuitive, unlike any other previous QUEST racing game (I tried and hated them all prior to this one).
It's addictive and I wish it's only the start of a franchise.

For DEVs:
However, few things don't work properly (yet): 
- it always disconnect from the online server, compared to other online games (ping tempo seem inconsistent).
- when it disconnects, I need to restart the game to access the multiplayer and online contests (even though the internet connection is good).
- the mixed reality option doesn't seem to work, and need to restart to get out of 'it'.
- in the online contests, my hoverbike 'style' is resetted to the original one.
Love the customization!

V-Racer Hoverbike delivers what VR racing fans have been waiting for: daring circuits, varied modes, and a powerful sense of speed. Technically and graphically, it's top-notch!
Between drifting, missiles, and multiplayer challenges, the game makes you want to start again and easily becomes addictive. Finally, the true Wipeout of VR!
What I really like is this balance between pure adrenaline and VR comfort.
We enter the races with stylish hoverbikes, we tilt or lean slightly to turn, we dodge mines or missiles, and it's fast-paced without any motion sickness issues.
The 30 circuits are diverse, which avoids redundancy.
Weapons add a tactical layer that competes with pure speed.
The "Survivor" mode is particularly intense: staying in the race while your opponents explode around us puts the pressure on. Cross-play with PCVR reinforces the social potential.
Some tracks are very demanding and take some time to get used to.
V-Racer Hoverbike is the fun, futuristic arcade racing game we've all been waiting for!
Game Mechanics:
Tilt steering to turn, brake, or accelerate.
Use weapons like missiles, mines, EMPs, or drones to interact with opponents.
Various game modes: pure races, survival mode, combat, and weekly challenges.
Driving skills, boost management, and trajectory anticipation are essential.

Love it. The control method works extremely well. I've only knocked one star off because I'm struggling with customising text and decals. There's a tutorial button but nothing plays?  Fix that and it's definitely 5 stars.

developer_response.body: You are right, the tutorial was broken in build 161. We have released a fix for this on 10/27. Sorry about the inconvenience.

reviews.review_description: Not too derp, but most fun ice had with a racing game in vr yet

reviews.review_title: The Best VR Racer Since Wipeout
reviews.review_description:
FULL REVIEW AVAILABLE ON GAMERTAG VR YOUTUBE CHANNEL

I almost passed this game up because VRacer first launched way back in 2018 on oculus rift and even though the sense of speed was good, I really didn’t feel it had much else to offer, but the developer has really put the work in for this updated new release and overall I’ve came away extremely impressed. Get in, get online and prepare to be blown away
Wipe out meets tron in this fast paced racer/shooter. Great game just wish it didn't make me feel so queasy! Hope that fades eventually so I can enjoy this quality title.
......just because. 🏍
Absolutely wonderful game. Thank you Devs! I haven't had so much fun since Wipeout Omega. Keep up the great work!

reviews.review_title: The Game That Made Me Yell "WHEEEEE"
reviews.review_description:
Move over, Mario Kart — there's a new chaos machine in town, and it flies. VRacer Hoverbike is basically what happens when you strap jet engines to a blender, toss in some rainbow sprinkles, and call it a sport. It’s Mario Kart meets Tron meets “why am I flying face-first into a wall while grinning like an idiot?”

The hoverbikes? Oh, they're fast. Like “I-just-blinked-and-hit-a-wall-at-300mph” fast. Competing with friends is the best part, especially when you ruin lifelong friendships with a well-timed power-up shaped like some kind of a disguised blue shell. Weekly leaderboards let you flex your virtual racing muscles, assuming you didn’t dislocate something during a particularly aggressive lean.

Speaking of leaning — I don’t use joysticks, because I’m not a peasant. I use my "chest." Yes, I race by physically throwing my torso left and right like I'm trying to seduce the bike with interpretive dance. It’s immersive. It’s athletic cause now I have a new six pack of abs. Not really. It’s probably very confusing to people watching me from the outside.

The career mode lets you earn cash by standing proudly on the podium, preferably while still catching your breath from the last loop-de-loop that made you question your VR legs. Use your winnings to upgrade your hoverbike into something that looks like it was designed by BoboTheClown on Red Bull. You can customize everything — your gear, your bike, probably even your dignity (mine is in the shop).

Graphics? Oh baby, they’re vivid. Like, "lick-the-rainbow" vivid. Over 30 tracks await, each packed with insane turns, gravity-defying nonsense, and loopy doopy spinny things that turn your brain into scrambled eggs. There's even a tutorial for all the steering options in case you want to pretend like you're not just winging it the whole time.

Best part? You never have to release the throttle. That’s right — never let go. Just slap the brakes while still full-throttle like the adrenaline-fueled maniac you are. It’s like riding a rocket while trying to parallel park.

Mastering the game is easy after a few practice laps, but don’t worry — you’ll still be pinballing off walls and cackling madly the whole time. Honestly, it's not just a racing game — it’s a full-body comedy routine. And I love every unhinged second of it.

Well I’ve been a biker for a long time and always thought a MotoGP style vr game would be a good thing to have in my library. I like the easy to get to grips with control setup. Love the arcadesque Mario kart power ups (wish we had more variety though) having the learning curve it has means I’m spending quite a bit of time in here to get my bike upgraded. Multiplayer is quite well implemented too. I had this on steam and was disappointed to see it get very little attention or updates, but this now on the quest 3 makes me see why that was. Hopefully the updates carryon here and we see more content 👍

My first racer for quest 3 

I can certainly see where the familiarity in people saying it's a cross between wipeout & Mario kart 👍 😍  

Started of using the lean but overall I find using analogues too turn better especially going up n over a loop 2 loop 🙃 😆 🤣  it's such fun 

No issues to report 👌 🙌 😌 

Big kuddos to all the team who's working on this one 👏
F zero meets Mario Kart

Game is great. But there’s ALOT of filler levels in vapRacing against the clock is just boring. That’s what time trials are for.  

Would be better to have more courses. 

Speaking of which, the courses in this game are BEAUTIFUL. Controls are great. The only issue is the filler levels.  

Hopefully there will be some updates with different courses.  Give us more big drops! Those were fun!

VRacer Hoverbike might as well be the most fun racing game I’ve played in VR! The entire game is incredibly polished and it’s great to see it on Quest. Controls are intuitive and the futuristic design really makes it feel like a spiritual successor to Wipeout. This game has given me a reason to bring my Quest with me when traveling to hop in a race every now and then during the evenings too! My only real suggestion would be some form of cross-save between Meta and Steam… I need this game on every VR platform in my life :)
'nuff said
very dope game
Great racing game and in my list of games, it had a Halloween themed content update. That gives more replayability and reasons to keep going back to play it.

This is easily the best racing I’ve played in VR. The controls are incredibly easy to learn, but difficult to master. The campaign mode is a blast. The tracks remind me of the good ole FZero/SNES days. The combat is a nice added touch too… damage done to your bike doesn’t take you out of the race like Mario kart… you get slowed down but it never feels unfair. I’m honestly loving this game. Totally addicted right now. 
The only complaint I have is multiplayer seems pretty dead… I’ve logged in twice (once during peak hours, dinner time) and not a soul was online. It’s a shame cuz this could be an amazing multiplayer racer. Hopefully over time it gets more populated. 

Other than that complaint, it’s a 10/10

developer_response.body: If you’re into multiplayer, get on our discord (Vertexbreakers). This is where players meet and organize for multi races

reviews.review_description: Apart from the great racing, the options are extensive and the whole onboarding process is slick. The complete racing package for Quest 3.

reviews.review_title: Awesome racing game,best on the Quest 3
reviews.review_description:
This is the fastest racing game I've played on the Quest 3 and 7 years of developing, it's polished and looks amazing.I didn't notice any bugs from what I played.

So immersive and the bikes look cool the weapons are great and the control's feel perfect.

The tracks are very well done and upgrades are great, earn cash and upgrade.

Career mode is well done and the difficulties in levels are well implemented and multiplayer is great too.

Definitely a must buy if you like racing games.

5 stars from me.

Yes, I rated 5 stars and still have some complaints. Game runs smooth, is fun, and deserves 5 stars. Here’s my only complaints: you may get motion sickness. I’ve read other reviews stating that you don’t and I don’t agree. It gives you a sense of speed. When the race comes to an end and the bike brakes to a halt, you will feel yourself going forward; so to say you won’t would be misleading. But it wasn’t terrible. 
  My last complaint is small. There’s no gear shifting. This is an arcade type game. It’s not trying to be a simulator but that would have been a nice touch. 
  Again, great game

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review! Indeed, you are right to mention it, we can NOT guarantee that every player will be fine with the game. What we hear is that the vast majority doesn't have any problems with it, but everyone has a different sensitivity to motion sickness.

reviews.review_description:
If you have some nostalgia for Wipeout series, this is the game, but with bikes... hoverbikes, to be more precise.
Great track design, basic and innovative racing modes, with bike, suit and helmet customization.
Because of the steering system, you won't get dizzy or have nausea.
Totally recommended!

This game is hands down the best racer on Quest 3. The controls are perfect and make you feel like you are really sitting on the hoverbike. Even though the tracks can be crazy I have never experienced vertigo while playing making this a great game to play until your battery runs out. Lots of modes and customization. Get this game!

Got this on a whim to see what it was like and haven't has this much fun in ages , the grafix are really good and the game play is cool . Two more of my friends have it now also and we have a great time racing each other on there and I have only meet nice players on there so my advice would be to get it and have some fun .

Edit: after the last update, the framerate is butter smooth, well done to the devs who made such a great game! 

Finally a proper racing game on the quest  I strongly advise the purchase, even if not discounted ( actually buy it not discounted, the devs deserve to get rewarded)

The gameplay with leaning left and right is just absolutely perfect, I haven't tried the other methods the game has because I just enjoy the leaning gameplay so much. I just increased the sensitivity a bit so i could turn to the max without having to lean too much. To describe it, you just hold your controllers flat on your legs in the position like it's the bike handles and you turn by leaning , it is not only very immersive but also super precise.

The game content is very good, different game mides similar to something you would find in a wipeout game. The star of the show is the multiplayer, for now i saw only chill people, no screaming kids , fingers crossed they don't find this game.

For the negatives, when you complete the solo race you can see that you already won gold , silver or bronze but i couldn't find anything that tells you if you achieved it in rookie mode or something more difficult. It is just completed. I was planning to redo everything in harder difficulty. 

Another point i hope would be improved is the framerate, you can feel the quest 3 is pushed to the limits with this game and sometime it can be a little bit choppy, very strangely it doesn't impact as much as it should the gameplay but if there was one point i would love the devs to improve it would be the priority n°1.

Just give this game a chance, do not let it be drown in the sea of very mediocre games the quest is plagued with, for once we got some quality content, give it as much love the devs seemed to have given this game. Buy, enjoy, give 5 stars.
If you liked fzero and without its a must
Bikevbike
